rulings.Case Title: WILLIAM PEARSON VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, July 22, 2008, line 1. DEFENDANT ESTATE OF EARL H. BARKER'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is sustained without leave to amend. The Court takes judicial notice of the Certificate of Dissolution. =(302/PJM)

rulings.Case Title: THIRD EYE BLIND,INC VS. NORTH AMERICAN SPECIALTY INSURANCE CO
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Good Faith Settlement And Req. For Judicial Ntc
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, July 22, 2008, line 17. CROSS DEFENDANTS DAVID RAWSON and ZIESLER ZEISLER & RAWSON L L P'S Motion For Good Faith Settlement is denied. The central issue is which entity had responsibility for the insurance needs of T E B, Inc. The parties have offered very different interpretations of the January 26, 1999 Godtland letter and the responsibilities thereafter of Provident and Z Z & R. The Court cannot say that one interpretation is more persuasive than the other. Further, the continuing relationship between Z Z & R and T E B, Inc raises a question as to the arms length nature of their negotiations. A settlement representing only 2 % of the potential maximum exposure does not satisfy Tech-Built. =(302/PJM/xMW)

rulings.Case Title: ARLO R HAUPT VS. A W CHESTERTON COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, July 22, 2008, line 16. DEFENDANT INGERSOLL-RAND COMPANY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is granted. Defendant shifts the burden of proof. Plaintiff's interrogatory responses reflect that disturbing asbestos containing gaskets and packing of defendant's products was "routinely performed" and a part of "regular upkeep and maintenance." In response to special interrogatory 17, plaintiff does not provide any factual basis that the products Mr. Haupt was exposed to contain original equipment. Plaintiff fails to present on facts that address Haupt's exposure to original equipment manufactured by defendant. =(302/PJM/VC)

rulings.Case Title: WILLIAM YOSHI VS. HAN ZHANG LI et al

rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, July 22, 2008, line 5. DEFENDANT HAN LI'S Motion To Set Aside Entry Of Default And Default Judgment is denied. Defendant fails to attach a proposed pleading, and the motion is untimely. =(302/PJM/AY)

rulings.Case Title: FLORO G ASUNCION VS. UNION CAB COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, July 22, 2008, line 6. DEFENDANTS LAM TU and JOHN NGUYEN'S DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT is overruled. Ten days to answer. Service of the complaint constitutes notice for recission, C C 1691. The agreements alleged to be with the company, alter ego and fraud are properly plead. =(302/PJM/AY)

rulings.Case Title: FLORO G ASUNCION VS. UNION CAB COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, July 22, 2008, line 6. DEFENDANTS LAM TU and JOHN NGUYEN'S MOTION TO STRIKE 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT is denied. The Court may not take judicial notice of the content of the deposition. The allegations of fraud in the complaint are sufficient to state a cause of action. =(302/PJM/AY)

rulings.Case Title: VADIMIR OSTANIN VS. INGENIO, INC., A DELAWARE CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO AMENDED ANSWER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, July 22, 2008, line 9. PLAINTIFF VADIMIR OSTANIN'S DEMURRER TO AMENDED ANSWER TO COMPLAINT. The demurrer is sustained to the 13 and 14 affirmative defenses, no opposition having been made. The demurrer is sustained as to the 18th cause of action for there is no such affirmative defense. The 4th affirmative defense alleges multiple grounds for relief. It is, as a consequence, uncertain and the Court is not aware of any authority that obligates plaintiff to advise defendant of a right to overtime. Sustained with 10 days leave to amend. The demurrer to the 5th affirmative defense, laches, is overruled. The demurrer to the 6th affirmative defense is sustained with 10 days leave to amend to plead facts that demonstrate a basis for the defense of unclean hands. The demurrer to the 7th affirmative defense is sustained. The facts alleged do not provide a basis for the alleged defense of failure to mitigate damages. Ten days leave to amend. =(302/PJM/xAM)
